subject: Geography
title: Son Doong: The World's Largest
description: A fast breakdown of Son Doong: The World's Largest for builders.

hook.text: Son Doong in Vietnam is the world's largest cave—big enough to fly a Boeing 747 through.

buildup.text: A local farmer found its entrance in 1991 but the roaring underground river scared him away.

discovery.text: British cavers explored it in 2009 and found a passage 5 km long and 200 meters tall.

twist.text: It has its own jungle inside—where the roof collapsed, trees grew in the sunlight.

climax.text: Son Doong has its own clouds, formed when moist air rises inside its enormous chambers.

punchline.text: A cave so big it makes its own weather.

quiz.question: What unusual feature does Son Doong Cave have?
options: Its own clouds and a jungle inside
